sentences.sentence: In fact, Newman believes the main reason for adopting one sort of transport over another is politics: 'The more democratic the process, the more public transport is favored.' He considers Portland, Oregon, a perfect example of this.
sentences.translation: 事实上，纽曼认为选择一种交通方式而非另一种的主要原因是政治：“决策过程越民主，公共交通越受青睐。”他认为俄勒冈州波特兰就是很好的例子。

structure.text: Newman believes
the main reason for adopting one sort of transport over another is politics
The more democratic the process, the more public transport is favored
He considers Portland, Oregon, a perfect example of this

difficult_points: the main reason...is politics 是 believe 的宾语从句。
The more..., the more... 表示‘越……越……’；considers 后接宾语补语。

expressions.text: adopt one sort of transport over another
expressions.meaning_zh: 选择一种交通而非另一种

sentences.sentence: Some years ago, federal money was granted to build a new road.
sentences.translation: 几年前，联邦政府拨款修建一条新道路。

structure.text: Some years ago
federal money was granted
to build a new road

difficult_points: was granted 是被动语态，主语是 federal money。
to build 表示拨款用途。

sentences.sentence: However, local pressure groups forced a referendum over whether to spend the money on light rail instead.
sentences.translation: 然而，当地压力团体迫使政府举行公投，决定是否把这笔钱改用于轻轨。

structure.text: local pressure groups
forced a referendum
over whether to spend the money on light rail instead

difficult_points: force + 宾语表示迫使举行公投。
whether to spend 是嵌入式选择问题，instead 表示改为。

sentences.sentence: The rail proposal won and the railway worked spectacularly well.
sentences.translation: 轻轨提案获胜，铁路运行得极其出色。

structure.text: The rail proposal won
and the railway worked spectacularly well

difficult_points: and 连接投票结果与铁路运行效果。
spectacularly well 是程度副词修饰 worked。

sentences.sentence: In the years that have followed, more and more rail systems have been put in, dramatically changing the nature of the city.
sentences.translation: 在随后的几年里，越来越多的铁路系统建成，极大改变了城市面貌。

structure.text: In the years that have followed
more and more rail systems have been put in
dramatically changing the nature of the city

difficult_points: that have followed 修饰 years。
have been put in 是现在完成时被动语态，changing 表示结果。

sentences.sentence: Newman notes that Portland has about the same population as Perth and had a similar population density at the time.
sentences.translation: 纽曼指出，波特兰人口与珀斯大致相同，当时人口密度也相近。

structure.text: Newman notes
that Portland has about the same population as Perth and had a similar population density at the time

difficult_points: that 从句中 has 与 had 并列。
the same...as... 比较人口，at the time 指当时。

sentences.sentence: In the UK, travel times to work had been stable for at least six centuries, with people avoiding situations that required them to spend more than half an hour travelling to work.
sentences.translation: 在英国，上班通勤时间至少六个世纪以来一直稳定，人们会避免需要花费超过半小时通勤的情况。

structure.text: In the UK
travel times to work had been stable for at least six centuries
with people avoiding situations that required them to spend more than half an hour travelling to work
that required them to spend more than half an hour travelling to work

difficult_points: with 复合结构补充人们的行为。
require + 宾语 + to do 表示需要他们花时间通勤。

sentences.sentence: Trains and cars initially allowed people to live at greater distances without taking longer to reach their destination.
sentences.translation: 起初，火车和汽车让人们能够住得更远，却不用花更长时间到达目的地。

structure.text: Trains and cars
initially allowed people to live at greater distances
without taking longer to reach their destination

difficult_points: allow + 宾语 + to do 表示允许或使能够。
without taking longer 表示没有增加耗时。

sentences.sentence: However, public infrastructure did not keep pace with urban sprawl, causing massive congestion problems which now make commuting times far higher.
sentences.translation: 然而，公共基础设施没有跟上城市扩张，造成严重拥堵，如今使通勤时间大幅增加。

structure.text: public infrastructure did not keep pace with urban sprawl
causing massive congestion problems
which now make commuting times far higher

difficult_points: keep pace with 表示跟上发展。
which 修饰 congestion problems，make + 宾语 + 形容词表示使变得更高。

sentences.sentence: There is a widespread belief that increasing wealth encourages people to live farther out where cars are the only viable transport.
sentences.translation: 人们普遍认为，财富增加会促使人们住得更远，在那里汽车是唯一可行的交通工具。

structure.text: There is a widespread belief
that increasing wealth encourages people to live farther out
where cars are the only viable transport

difficult_points: that 从句说明 belief 内容。
where 引导地点从句，viable 表示可行。

sentences.sentence: The example of European cities refutes that.
sentences.translation: 欧洲城市的例子反驳了这一点。

structure.text: The example of European cities
refutes that

difficult_points: that 指上一句关于财富和汽车的看法。
refute 表示用证据驳斥。

sentences.sentence: They are often wealthier than their American counterparts but have not generated the same level of car use.
sentences.translation: 它们通常比美国同类城市更富裕，却没有产生同等程度的汽车使用。

structure.text: They are often wealthier than their American counterparts
but have not generated the same level of car use

difficult_points: They 指 European cities。
but 对比财富水平和汽车使用程度。

sentences.sentence: In Stockholm, car use has actually fallen in recent years as the city has become larger and wealthier.
sentences.translation: 在斯德哥尔摩，近年来汽车使用量反而下降，尽管城市变得更大、更富裕。

structure.text: In Stockholm
car use has actually fallen in recent years
as the city has become larger and wealthier

difficult_points: as 表示伴随变化，两个 has become 后接比较级。
actually 强调结果出乎预期。

sentences.sentence: A new study makes this point even more starkly.
sentences.translation: 一项新研究更加鲜明地说明了这一点。

structure.text: A new study
makes this point even more starkly

difficult_points: this point 指欧洲城市财富与汽车使用的反差。
even more starkly 表示更加明显。

sentences.sentence: Developing cities in Asia, such as Jakarta and Bangkok, make more use of the car than wealthy Asian cities such as Tokyo and Singapore.
sentences.translation: 亚洲的发展中城市，如雅加达和曼谷，比东京、新加坡等富裕亚洲城市更依赖汽车。

structure.text: Developing cities in Asia, such as Jakarta and Bangkok
make more use of the car than wealthy Asian cities such as Tokyo and Singapore

difficult_points: such as 两次列举比较的城市。
make more use of...than... 表示使用程度比较。

sentences.sentence: In cities that developed later, the World Bank and Asian Development Bank discouraged the building of public transport and people have been forced to rely on cars - creating the massive traffic jams that characterize those cities.
sentences.translation: 在发展较晚的城市，世界银行和亚洲开发银行不鼓励建设公共交通，人们被迫依赖汽车，从而造成了这些城市特有的严重堵车。

structure.text: In cities that developed later
the World Bank and Asian Development Bank discouraged the building of public transport
people have been forced to rely on cars
creating the massive traffic jams that characterize those cities
that characterize those cities

difficult_points: that developed later 修饰 cities。
have been forced to 是现在完成时被动；creating 表示结果。

sentences.sentence: Newman believes one of the best studies on how cities built for cars might be converted to rail use is The Urban Village report, which used Melbourne as an example.
sentences.translation: 纽曼认为，关于汽车城市如何转为铁路使用的最佳研究之一是《城市村庄》报告，该报告以墨尔本为例。

structure.text: Newman believes
one of the best studies on how cities built for cars might be converted to rail use is The Urban Village report
how cities built for cars might be converted to rail use
which used Melbourne as an example

difficult_points: how 从句嵌在 study 名词短语中；built for cars 修饰 cities。
which 修饰 report；use A as an example 表示以 A 为例。

sentences.sentence: It found that pushing everyone into the city centre was not the best approach.
sentences.translation: 报告发现，把所有人都集中到市中心并不是最佳做法。

structure.text: It found
that pushing everyone into the city centre was not the best approach
pushing everyone into the city centre

difficult_points: pushing 动名词短语作宾语从句主语。
that 从句说明报告结论。

sentences.sentence: Instead, the proposal advocated the creation of urban villages at hundreds of sites, mostly around railway stations.
sentences.translation: 相反，该提案倡导在数百个地点建设城市村庄，主要分布在火车站周边。

structure.text: Instead
the proposal advocated the creation of urban villages at hundreds of sites
mostly around railway stations

difficult_points: Instead 表示与集中市中心相反的方案。
advocate 后接名词 the creation。

sentences.sentence: It was once assumed that improvements in telecommunications would lead to more dispersal in the population as people were no longer forced into cities.
sentences.translation: 人们曾认为，电信进步会使人口更加分散，因为人们不再被迫进入城市。

structure.text: It was once assumed
that improvements in telecommunications would lead to more dispersal in the population
as people were no longer forced into cities

difficult_points: It 是形式主语，that 从句是真正内容。
as 从句说明人口分散的原因；were forced 是被动。

sentences.sentence: However, the ISTP team's research demonstrates that the population and job density of cities rose or remained constant in the 1980s after decades of decline.
sentences.translation: 然而，ISTP团队的研究表明，经历数十年下降后，城市人口和就业密度在20世纪80年代上升或保持不变。

structure.text: the ISTP team's research demonstrates
that the population and job density of cities rose or remained constant in the 1980s after decades of decline

difficult_points: that 从句中 rose 与 remained 并列。
after decades of decline 表示此前趋势。

sentences.sentence: The explanation for this seems to be that it is valuable to place people working in related fields together.
sentences.translation: 对此的解释似乎是，把从事相关领域工作的人聚集在一起很有价值。

structure.text: The explanation for this
seems to be
that it is valuable to place people working in related fields together
working in related fields

difficult_points: that 从句作表语；it 是形式主语。
working in related fields 修饰 people，to place 表示真正主语内容。

sentences.sentence: 'The new world will largely depend on human creativity, and creativity flourishes where people come together face-to-face.'
sentences.translation: “新世界将在很大程度上依赖人类创造力，而人们面对面聚集的地方，创造力就会蓬勃发展。”

structure.text: The new world will largely depend on human creativity
and creativity flourishes where people come together face-to-face
where people come together face-to-face

difficult_points: and 连接依赖创造力与创造力发展的两个分句。
where 引导地点从句，face-to-face 表示面对面。
